mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows users to manage security, compliance, and governance through policy-as-code. It simplifies policy management without requiring a new language, making it accessible for platform engineering teams. Key trade-offs include the need for careful policy design to avoid unintended consequences on resource management.

steps.title: Add the Kyverno Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kyverno Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install Kyverno easily.

```bash

helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno/

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Kyverno repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kyverno CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Kyverno to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kyverno/kyverno/v1.17.1/config/release/kyverno-crds.yaml

```

> This applies the CRDs from the specified version.

steps.title: Install Kyverno using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kyverno policy engine into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --version 1.17.1 --namespace kyverno --create-namespace

```

> Installs Kyverno in the 'kyverno' namespace.

steps.title: Verify Kyverno installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kyverno pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> This command lists all pods in the kyverno namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create an example policy
steps.description:
This step creates a sample policy to demonstrate Kyverno's functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

```

> This creates a policy that requires the 'app' label on Pods.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF

steps.title: Verify the policy is applied
steps.description:
This step checks if the policy is active and ready to enforce rules.

```bash

kubectl get cpol require-labels -o yaml -n kyverno

```

> This command retrieves the created policy to verify its configuration.
commands: kubectl get cpol require-labels -o yaml -n kyverno

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kyverno from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all policies and associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kyverno/kyverno/v1.17.1/config/release/kyverno-crds.yaml

```

> This command uninstalls the Kyverno release.
> This command removes the CRDs associated with Kyverno.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your policies. This step involves draining the Kyverno pods, upgrading the installation, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <kyverno-pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --version 1.17.1 --namespace kyverno

kubectl uncordon <kyverno-pod-name>

kubectl get pods -n kyverno

```

> Replace <kyverno-pod-name> with the actual pod name to drain.
> This command upgrades Kyverno to the specified version.
> Replace <kyverno-pod-name> with the actual pod name to uncordon.
> Verify that the Kyverno pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to validate resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when a resource does not meet the validation criteria defined in a policy.

**Fix:**
Review the policy and ensure that the resource being created meets the specified requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration in the policies or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kyverno pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kyverno' and adjust the policies or resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Policy not enforcing as expected
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The policy might not be applied correctly or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the policy is created in the correct namespace and check for any typos in the policy definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to find CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the required Custom Resource Definitions are not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kyverno/kyverno/v1.17.1/config/release/kyverno-crds.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new validation rules, improved performance for policy evaluation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see the Kyverno pods running with a status of 'Running' and the example policy applied successfully, as indicated by 'kubectl get cpol require-labels -o yaml -n kyverno'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  rules:
    - name: check-labels
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
EOF
kubectl get cpol require-labels -o yaml -n kyverno

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
